title: Distichorchis refracta
taxonomy: Distichorchis refracta is classified within the family Orchidaceae, commonly known as the orchid family. This classification places it among the largest and most diverse groups of flowering plants. The genus Distichorchis is further grouped under the subfamily Orchidoideae, which includes several genera of terrestrial orchids. Distichorchis refracta is native to New Guinea and is considered an endemic species found in this region. It is often classified within the tribe Coelogyneae, which comprises a range of terrestrial orchids.

intro: Distichorchis refracta, also known as the 'Ribbon Orchid', is a flowering plant species native to Australia. The health benefits of Distichorchis refracta include its potential to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the treatment of various infections and inflammatory conditions.
